goal: Improve fast-charging durability for LFP EV batteries

hypothesis.title: Pulse charging plus additive-stabilized SEI for LFP
hypothesis.hypothesis: Combining pulse-rest charging with SEI-forming additives will improve LFP capacity retention under 3C to 4C charging.

notes: Good multi-evidence hypothesis with explicit contradiction coverage. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Improve cold fast charging safety for LFP EV cells

hypothesis.title: Cold plating diagnostic adaptive cutoff
hypothesis.hypothesis: Voltage relaxation and differential-voltage signals can guide adaptive current cutoffs to reduce lithium plating during cold LFP fast charging.

notes: Strong support for diagnostics plus controls, with cold-SEI limitation noted. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Improve LFP fast charging through electrolyte additives

hypothesis.title: Additive-only LFP durability hypothesis
hypothesis.hypothesis: A fluorinated additive package will improve LFP fast-charge retention.

notes: Weak but not totally unsupported; should not be recommended without more support. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Apply silicon anode insight to LFP fast charging

hypothesis.title: Silicon swelling result transferred to LFP
hypothesis.hypothesis: Silicon graphite swelling evidence proves that LFP graphite cells will suffer identical swelling under 3C charging.

notes: Domain-transfer overclaim; should be high risk and revised. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Use solid-state separators for commercial LFP fast charging

hypothesis.title: Separator screening to commercial LFP overclaim
hypothesis.hypothesis: Ceramic-rich separators are ready to solve commercial LFP EV fast charging safety.

notes: Claim explicitly says not demonstrated in commercial LFP EV cells. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Reduce fast-charge thermal degradation

hypothesis.title: Thermal uniformity plus adaptive charging
hypothesis.hypothesis: Reducing thermal gradients while shaping current should reduce impedance heterogeneity during fast charging.

notes: Reasonable cross-lever engineering hypothesis. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Eliminate lithium plating with SEI modifiers

hypothesis.title: SEI modifiers eliminate cold plating
hypothesis.hypothesis: SEI modifiers consistently eliminate lithium plating below 15 C in graphite cells.

notes: Contradicts claim:C010 but does not cite it. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Improve charge time and durability

hypothesis.title: Rest intervals without charge-time tradeoff
hypothesis.hypothesis: Longer rest intervals improve capacity retention with no charge-time penalty.

notes: Overclaim directly conflicts with limitation in cited claim. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Improve LFP fast charging controls

hypothesis.title: Adaptive current shaping improves cycle life
hypothesis.hypothesis: Voltage-feature-driven adaptive current shaping can improve LFP cycle life relative to fixed-current charging.

notes: Grounded but thinly supported; should be revised rather than advanced. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Combine diagnostics and additives for cold LFP fast charging

hypothesis.title: Diagnostic-gated additive protocol
hypothesis.hypothesis: Combining plating diagnostics with additive-stabilized interphases may reduce cold fast-charge degradation while preserving room-temperature LFP durability.

notes: Good hypothesis because it uses cautious language and includes contradiction coverage. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Improve LFP durability with protocol and controls

hypothesis.title: Pulse-rest plus adaptive shaping
hypothesis.hypothesis: Pulse-rest charging and voltage-feature-driven current shaping may reduce impedance growth and improve LFP cycle life.

notes: Good protocol/control hypothesis with charge-time tradeoff. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Improve additive-based LFP fast charging

hypothesis.title: Additive and SEI impedance package
hypothesis.hypothesis: A fluorinated additive package may improve capacity retention and lower charge-transfer resistance in high-rate LFP cells.

notes: Good additive/SEI hypothesis with low-temperature contradiction. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Design cold fast-charge diagnostic policy

hypothesis.title: Relaxation-aware cold charge policy
hypothesis.hypothesis: Voltage relaxation diagnostics may identify cold lithium plating onset and inform safer LFP charge cutoffs.

notes: Good diagnostics plus control hypothesis. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Improve thermal uniformity for fast charging

hypothesis.title: Thermal-gradient-aware protocol
hypothesis.hypothesis: Controlling thermal gradients during high-power charging may reduce local impedance growth in pouch-format cells.

notes: Good but format-transfer caveat remains. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Avoid cold plating in graphite fast charging

hypothesis.title: SEI additives guarantee cold plating suppression
hypothesis.hypothesis: SEI stabilizers prove that graphite cells will not plate lithium during cold fast charging.

notes: Overclaim ignores mixed outcomes below 15 C. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Transfer separator screening to LFP packs

hypothesis.title: Separator result proves EV pack safety
hypothesis.hypothesis: Ceramic-rich separator screening proves commercial LFP EV packs can fast charge safely at high current.

notes: Commercial transfer overclaim. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Use silicon anode data for LFP

hypothesis.title: Silicon data maps directly to LFP
hypothesis.hypothesis: Silicon graphite swelling results prove LFP graphite cells have the same high-rate aging mechanism.

notes: Chemistry transfer risk. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Improve rest interval protocol

hypothesis.title: Long rest intervals are free durability gains
hypothesis.hypothesis: Longer rest intervals improve retention with no practical EV usability tradeoff.

notes: Ignores charge-time penalty. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Control LFP fast charging by current shaping

hypothesis.title: Current shaping alone is enough
hypothesis.hypothesis: Voltage-feature-driven adaptive current shaping alone is sufficient to solve LFP fast-charge degradation.

notes: Thin support and overbroad solution language. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Build additive and protocol hypothesis

hypothesis.title: Additive plus pulse-rest cautious proposal
hypothesis.hypothesis: Combining pulse-rest charging with fluorinated additive-derived interphase stabilization may reduce impedance growth while preserving LFP capacity retention.

notes: Good cautious combined hypothesis. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Study room temperature vs cold fast charging

hypothesis.title: Room-temperature protocol generalized to cold
hypothesis.hypothesis: Pulse-rest charging tested at 25 C proves the protocol will prevent cold lithium plating.

notes: Temperature mismatch and unsupported plating claim. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Evaluate pouch-cell additive readiness

hypothesis.title: Additive ready for large-format pouch cells
hypothesis.hypothesis: The fluorinated additive blend is ready for large-format pouch-cell deployment with no gas-evolution risk.

notes: Directly conflicts with unmeasured gas-evolution limitation. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Use plating diagnostics in aged cells

hypothesis.title: Aging-robust plating diagnostics
hypothesis.hypothesis: Differential-voltage signatures are reliable for plating detection even in highly aged LFP cells.

notes: Directly contradicts reliability limitation. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Engineer diagnostic-gated fast charging

hypothesis.title: Diagnostics plus adaptive control
hypothesis.hypothesis: Pairing plating diagnostics with adaptive current shaping may reduce cold-charge risk while maintaining LFP cycle-life benefits.

notes: Good diagnostic/control hypothesis. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Avoid pack-level thermal overclaim

hypothesis.title: Pouch mapping solves pack thermal control
hypothesis.hypothesis: Pouch-cell thermal mapping proves pack-level coolant control will eliminate fast-charge impedance heterogeneity.

notes: Format and deployment overclaim. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Combine SEI review and additive experiments

hypothesis.title: SEI evidence-backed additive screen
hypothesis.hypothesis: A targeted additive screen should prioritize formulations that lower charge-transfer resistance and maintain capacity retention under 3C LFP charging.

notes: Good experiment-oriented hypothesis. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Check controller limitations

hypothesis.title: Controller includes plating diagnostics
hypothesis.hypothesis: The adaptive current shaping controller already includes explicit lithium plating diagnostics.

notes: Directly conflicts with claim limitation. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Use solid-state adjacent safety insight cautiously

hypothesis.title: Separator concept as adjacent safety screen
hypothesis.hypothesis: Ceramic-rich separator concepts may be worth screening as adjacent safety controls, but commercial LFP validation is still required.

notes: Cautious but still adjacent-domain; should be revised or held. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Use thermal and additive evidence together

hypothesis.title: Thermal-additive interface stabilization
hypothesis.hypothesis: Reducing thermal hot spots while using additive-derived SEI stabilization may lower local impedance growth during high-rate LFP cycling.

notes: Good cross-lever hypothesis with format caveat. Generated expansion variant 1.

goal: Avoid causal language from review-only evidence

hypothesis.title: Review evidence proves SEI modifiers work universally
hypothesis.hypothesis: The SEI review proves all graphite fast-charge cells will have 30% lower impedance rise with modifiers.

notes: Overgeneralizes review range and ignores inconsistent baselines. Generated expansion variant 1.
